title: Escrow Deposit Requirements and Compliance for Licensed Recruitment Agencies
content:
SECTION 17. Maintenance of Escrow Deposit.- A licensed recruitment agency shall maintain at all times its escrow deposit in the minimum amount of One Million Pesos (PhP1,000,000.00). If required an additional escrow pursuant to Section 15, it shall maintain the escrow deposit of One Million Pesos plus the additional escrow. If the escrow deposit has been garnished, the agency must replenish it within fifteen (15) days from receipt of the Notice to Replenish Escrow Deposit. Failure to comply results in suspension of the license. The agency may explain within the same period why it should not be suspended. The Administration shall ensure compliance with the increased escrow deposit as provided.

SECTION 18. Monitoring Compliance with Conditions for the Issuance of the License.- The Administration shall monitor licensed recruitment agencies' compliance with license conditions, recruitment laws, rules and regulations, and labor and occupational safety standards in coordination with the DOLE Regional Office. The Administration may suspend or revoke the license for failure to maintain required qualifications or conditions.

SECTION 19. Release of Deposit in Escrow.- The escrow deposit of a licensed recruitment agency that voluntarily surrenders or does not renew its license shall be released upon submission of: (a) Copy of duly executed/signed escrow agreement; (b) Bank certification on escrow deposit status; (c) Certificate of No Pending Case from the NLRC; (d) Clearance from the Adjudication Office; (e) Notarized Board/Partnership Resolution received by the SEC indicating the person authorized to process and receive the escrow deposit check; and (f) Surety bond equivalent to the escrow deposit amount valid for four (4) years from license expiration, if applicable.
